feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: What SAP roles are required for an user to access /sap/xi/engine from CPI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have been migration our PO interfaces to CPI and we are using Basic authentication to connect with SAP systems.

While migrating proxy interfaces we are facing Authorization issues. The user we are using for all other communications is giving error '401- Unauthorized' when tried to access /sap/xi/engine.

What roles are required here? what options are available?

entry.title: how to extract a portion of the string from the given string
entry.content:
Hi 

Please help me that how can I extract portion of a string from the main string.

For example my input string: hello_world_howareyou.

We want to extract the word "world" but it is not fixed length. The substring is not working here.

Is there any other built-in  function.

Please help me on this.

Thanks

entry.title: How to check the particular field value in the API response and do mapping
entry.content: After doing message mapping, the output value (XYZ) of a particular field (user) should match with the field(group) value (xyz) in the output response of an API. How to find the corresponding record from the API response and map the "id" of that record to this another field.

entry.title: IDOC xsd import giving error in message mapping in SAP CPI iflow
entry.content:
Hi experts,
I am trying to upload the IDOC xsd (from PO ->IDOC ->xsd) but i am getting the error on attributes and also on idoc control segment field
Reference - IDOC xsd import error.png
 
I then came across the below post
CPI Message Mapping XSD Error
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cpi-message-mapping-xsd-error/qaq-p/12048997
 
I tried to follow the steps as suggested -
Downloaded the XML Schema at http://www.w3.org/2001/xml.xsd and uploaded it as a resource to the iflow
the IDOC xsd does not have any reference and no import 
But tried it anyways which i think is the wrong location anyways and it gave error as expected -<xsd:schema xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" schemaLocation="xml.xsd"/>
 
I have searched and i am unable to find why an IDOC xsd generated from PO is not getting uploaded correctly
I can view it correctly and the xsd is valid
 
Please help me as i need to be able to upload the xsd for my message mapping

entry.title: Basic Authentication Fails with “Bad Credentials” When Calling SAP Integration Flow Endpoint
entry.content: I have created an integration flow using HTTPS and registered the service instance. I am using the API endpoint generated by the integration flow and have assigned the service keys. In Postman, I am attempting to authenticate using Basic Authentication with the client ID and client secret from the service key. However, Postman returns a “Bad credentials” error. What can cause that issue?

entry.title: Bulk Record Insertion into Database Using Groovy Script in CPI
entry.content:
Bulk Record Insertion into Database Using Groovy Script in CPI

  • Bulk records can be inserted into a database from SAP CPI using a Groovy script that converts XML input data into SQL insert queries for JDBC execution .
  • A sample scenario involves three employee records with unique IDs and names, passed as XML payload to the Groovy script for processing .

Groovy Script Functionality and Workflow

  • The Groovy script reads the input XML containing multiple employee records and iterates over each record using a loop to generate individual SQL insert statements 
     
  • The script constructs insert queries dynamically by appending SQL syntax and field values extracted from the XML, handling both string and numeric data types appropriately  .
  • String values are enclosed in single quotes in the SQL query, while numeric values are inserted without quotes to ensure correct data typing in the database .
  • This looping and appending process repeats for all records in the XML, creating a batch of insert queries for bulk insertion 
     

Integration with CPI and JDBC Adapter

  • The Groovy script outputs the complete SQL insert queries, which are then executed by the JDBC adapter configured with a database alias in CPI to insert data into the target database table .
  • The process does not require explicit mapping in CPI since the Groovy script handles the conversion from XML to SQL queries directly  .
  • The example flow uses a scheduler to trigger the job and a content modifier to provide the XML payload as input to the Groovy script  .

Verification and Results

  • Before insertion, the database is checked to confirm that the employee records with the specified IDs do not already exist, ensuring no duplicates.
  • After running the flow, the Groovy script successfully inserts all employee records in bulk into the database, verified by querying the database and confirming the presence of the new records 
     

Practical Implications and Usage

  • This approach allows efficient bulk insertion of records in SAP CPI, reducing the overhead of inserting records one by one and improving performance.
  • The Groovy script is reusable for different XML inputs and can be adapted for various bulk database insertion requirements in CPI integration projects 
     
ℹ️ Note: Using Groovy scripting to generate SQL insert queries enables dynamic handling of input data structures and types, facilitating flexible and scalable database operations within CPI integration flows.
 
SAP Cloud for Customer integration with SAP S/4HANA  Cloud Integration SAP Cloud Integration for data services SAP Cloud for Customer, server-side integration

entry.title: Credit Note Adjustment through API in Public cloud
entry.content:
Sabari has various credit note adjustment scenarios being received against customer invoices. This data is being received from their internal DMS application via CP and being posted into SAP through standard APIs. There are many scenarios on how the data can be received:

  • One credit note adjusting against one customer invoice
  • One credit note adjusting against multiple invoices of the same customer
  • One invoice adjusting against multiple credit notes of the same customer

When the data is received, it is split into separate sets, and each set is individually processed and posted to SAP. We found that the same credit note or invoice may be duplicated in different scenarios. Hence, the Customer Account has to be cleared in SAP after each scenario is processed, to ensure the exact balances are reflected.

Sabari is receiving data from multiple DMS systems (different Supplier Dealer Management Systems), and to process the Credit Note Adjustment data for 1 supplier and 1 day, they have to clear the customer accounts at least 4 times for each Business units ( 6 Business units x 4 times = 24 times manual clearing on  a daily basis) . This is too much manual process at the moment, and we are looking at the best options to automate this. 

APIs

Thanks,

Vignesh

entry.title: Trace mode in Integration Suite is not working
entry.content:
Even after changing the log level to Trace in the IFlow, it remains at Info and I cannot view the logs.

 

I have undeployed and redeployed the IFlow, and changed the log level to Trace again, but nothing has changed.

I have also tried using a different browser and clearing the cache, but it had no effect.

Please check and advise.

entry.title: [CPI] HTTP "Don't Throw Exception on Failure" doesn't work properly on XI Sender Synch
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a synch XI Sender scenario, where the ERP sends a message to the CI, which makes an HTTPS request (with the option "Don't Throw Exception on Failure" enabled), and receives an HTTP Code 400 with a payload informing the reason for the error. I need delivery this message to ERP.
Analyzing the flow in the CI monitors, I don't see any errors, everything occurs as it should. However, when delivering the return message in the XI channel, an exception is generated on ERP, on proxy call.
This happens for example for HTTP Codes 400 and 404, but works perfectly for HTTP Code 500.
The payload received on 400 and 404, is shown on SXI_MONITOR as HTMLError, and no as MainDocument, as expected.

I think this is not happening as expected.
Has anyone noticed this behavior? Any known solutions?

Thanks,
Miguel Motta

entry.title: EDI in S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi All

IDOCs were used for EDI mapping and communication with Business Partners in S4HANA Private Cloud and older versions of SAP ERP. 

What is the recommended way to map EDIs for S4HANA Public Cloud? Are IDOCs available or can we use APIs for the mapping?

Regards

Lakshmi

entry.title: C4C V2 <> S4 Public Prospect to Account confirmation error
entry.content:
Hello Community,
We are working on integrating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with S/4HANA Public Cloud, using SAP Cloud Integration (CI) as the middleware.
When we convert a prospect to an account, the data is successfully replicated to S/4HANA Public Cloud — we can see the customer created in the target system. However, we are not receiving any confirmation back, and the integration fails in CI.
Upon investigation, it appears that S/4HANA Public Cloud is sending a SOAP message, whereas the pre-delivered SAP iFlow expects a JSON payload in the message mapping. This mismatch is causing the failure.

Error: com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occurred: A JSONObject text must begin with '{' at 1 [character 2 line 1].
Has anyone else encountered this issue?
Do we need to raise an SAP incident for this, or is there a known workaround or configuration that we might be missing?
Looking forward to your input. 

Best regards, Aditya

entry.title: How to capture the message content in a Exception sub-process?
entry.content:
Hello Fellow Members, 

With reference to the attached typical Exception payload caught in my Integration Flow, could you suggest a method by which i can save the <message> tag's content and update CBO's text field?

I tried using a Content Modifier and saving the content by using a XPATH: error/innererror/errordetails/errordetail/message, but for unknown reasons it always adds a empty string value to that CBO's text field. 

SAP Integration Suite SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: CI odata adapter: Edm.Guid 'Wrong literal format for literal'
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I'm building an iFlow using oData adapters (v2) in integration suite. When trying to update (PUT) the record adapter returns error:

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Wrong literal format for literal: 'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]'

I tried to workaround this problem by:

  • using batch (same error);
  • changing local edmx file and GUID type field from Edm.Guid to Edm.String and using guid'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]' (remote validation fails with Cannot convert guid'f5cc9649-...' to java.util.UUID
  • Playing with Resource Path in adapter's config - changing it to ${property.path} and setting hardcoded value: entityName(GuID=guid'f292992-aaaa-...') but same metadata validation

GUID is defined as:

<Key>
<PropertyRef Name="GuID"/>
</Key>
<Property Name="GuID" Type="Edm.Guid" Nullable="false" sap:display-format="UpperCase" sap:label="UUID" sap:quickinfo="16-byte UID in 32 chars (hexadecimal)" sap:updatable="false"/>

And using it manually (postman) is possible and works. I found that CI produces incorrect requests (in batch), skiping 'guid' part in key definition. Example batch requests, looks like this:

PUT entityName(GuID='f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]') HTTP/1.1

 instead of (which would work):

PUT entityName(GuID=guid'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]') HTTP/1.1

So either key validation fails in adapter or (with batch) in backend. How to make it work? I tried sap.support and adapter's configuration but there's nothing about such problems. 

Here's my payload I'm testing with:

<entitySetName>
<entityName>
<GuID>f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]</GuID>
<ProcessedAt>1990-01-26T06:40:03.02</ProcessedAt>
</entityName>
</entitySetName>

And adapter's config:

entry.title: BTP Integration Suite sending api_key in outbound REST call
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm having an HTTP 401 error calling a REST API that requires an "api_key" in the header using BTP Integration Suite Iflow, I've simulated the same with postman by directly putting the "api_key" and its value in the header request and it's working there.

Below is the Iflow that does the following process:

1. Set the user/password in the message body prior login api

2.Call the login api and retrieve the token

3.Convert json response into XML to retrieve the token and set it into the message header as api_key

4. Call  the getLogAPI <-- HTTP 401 issue

 

 

Below is a trace of message monitoring in BTP (blanked out the api_key):

 

 

Below is the postman request (blanked out the api_key):

 

 

Can someone advise how this should be handled?

entry.title: Integration Process to send the Multiple incoming payloads as a single file
entry.content:
Hi All,

Currently I'm facing issue with an integration process which is developed in PI/PO.

In that PI/PO integration process the incoming payloads are collected and grouped based on the "LogicalSystem" element present in the incoming payload. This flow triggers automatically when the message count reaches to 250 or wait time = 5 mins. Then grouped messages are passed to a message mapping to convert them into single XML file sends to the target system. 

How can implement this integration process in SAP CPI. Kindly help me with Process 

Thanks in advance

Regards

Rahul

entry.title: Unable to deploy value map - Error: Select default value for Source/Target - Cloud foundry
entry.content:
Error shown when trying to deploy artifact: Select default value for Source/Target. 

This only happens in our production environment. Dev and QA we are able to deploy.
The only difference in prod is we have configured values, as the production values are different to test environment.

Any advice is welcomed. 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Comparing Two XML Element Groups Using XSLT: A Practical Approach for Data Synchronization
entry.content:
In today’s enterprise integration landscape, maintaining data consistency across systems is a recurring challenge. Whether integrating SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, or third-party applications via SAP Cloud Integration, comparing two XML payloads to detect changes is a critical task.

This blog post walks through a common integration scenario: comparing two XML structures using XSLT to identify what has changed and needs to be synchronized. We'll detect added and updated elements and generate a clean delta XML for downstream processing.

📘 Use Case

Imagine the following XML document representing two data sources:

 

We aim to compare R1 (source) and User (target) to detect:

  • Fields in R1 missing in User → flagged as ADD

  • Fields in both, but with different values → flagged as UPDATE

  • Nested elements like Address are also compared deeply

🛠 XSLT Logic

Below is the complete XSLT 1.0 code that performs the comparison and outputs the results in a <Root><R2> structure. This can be used in SAP Cloud Integration’s message mappings, PI/PO mappings, or any tool that supports XSLT.

✅ Full XSLT Code

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0"
  xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
  
  <xsl:output method="xml" indent="yes"/>

  <xsl:template match="/Main">
    <Root>
      <R2>
        <xsl:variable name="r1" select="R1"/>
        <xsl:variable name="r2" select="User"/>

        <!-- Add operation: elements present in R1 but not in User -->
        <xsl:for-each select="$r1/*">
          <xsl:variable name="name" select="name()"/>
          <xsl:if test="not($r2/*[name() = $name])">
            <Add>
              <operation>ADD</operation>
              <xsl:copy-of select="."/>
            </Add>
          </xsl:if>
        </xsl:for-each>

        <!-- Update operation -->
        <xsl:for-each select="$r1/*">
          <xsl:variable name="name" select="name()"/>
          <xsl:variable name="r1node" select="."/>
          <xsl:variable name="r2node" select="$r2/*[name() = $name]"/>

          <!-- Direct value changes (non-nested) -->
          <xsl:if test="$r2node and not($r1node/*) and normalize-space($r1node) != normalize-space($r2node)">
            <update>
              <operation>Update</operation>
              <xsl:copy-of select="$r1node"/>
            </update>
          </xsl:if>

          <!-- Nested structure comparison -->
          <xsl:if test="$r1node/* and $r2node/*">
            <xsl:variable name="hasDifference">
              <xsl:for-each select="$r1node/*">
                <xsl:variable name="subname" select="name()"/>
                <xsl:variable name="r1sub" select="."/>
                <xsl:variable name="r2sub" select="$r2node/*[name() = $subname]"/>
                <xsl:if test="normalize-space($r1sub) != normalize-space($r2sub)">
                  <xsl:text>yes</xsl:text>
                </xsl:if>
              </xsl:for-each>
            </xsl:variable>
            <xsl:if test="contains($hasDifference, 'yes')">
              <update>
                <operation>Update</operation>
                <xsl:copy-of select="$r1node"/>
              </update>
            </xsl:if>
          </xsl:if>
        </xsl:for-each>
      </R2>
    </Root>
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

🧾 Sample Output

With the above input and XSLT, the result is:

This output structure is ideal for triggering update APIs, generating change logs, or sending notification messages.


Sure! Here's a revised version of your closing paragraph, tailored specifically to the context of the XML element group comparison post:

If you're working with XML transformations in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) and need to identify differences between nested XML structures, this XSLT-based approach can save you hours of manual comparison and debugging.

Have you encountered similar scenarios in CPI where comparing XML payloads was necessary? Share your solutions, questions, or challenges in the comments—let’s learn from each other!

entry.title: Duplicate file check in sFTP adapter BTP cloud integration
entry.content:
Hello all,

In SAP PI world we had duplicate file check option at adapter level for both NFS and sFTP.

We are trying to achieve the same thing in sFTP adapter in CPI but we do not have direct option available.

If anyone has done this scenario, can you please help us how to achieve this in CPI.

entry.title: Trigger to CPI when transport is moved from one instance to other using STMS.
entry.content: In SAP system we are using STMS transport mechanism. When transport is moved from Dev to Quality, we need trigger to CPI iflow where we need details like transport details. Any idea how to achieve this?

entry.title: Need help with SAP MAG - XSLT mapping context
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue while populating target field in MAG. E.g.  I have kept one-to-one mapping for //E1EDl24/POSNR --> //HL-I/LIN/350 . When E1EDL4 appearing only one time then working fine if E1EDL24 appearing multiple times then //HL-I/LIN/350 populating with all the source values (POSNR). Please suggest how to fix this.

Target XML like below , when it should contain 000010 in the first occurrence and 000020 in the 2nd.
